LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT EAN HOLDINGS, LLC'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT AS TO THE SOLE CAUSE OF ACTION FOR MOTOR VEHICLE NEGLIGENCE AGAINST IT IS SUSTAINED WITH TWENTY DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. EAN HOLDINGS' REQUEST FOR JUDICIAL NOTICE IS GRANTED. IF HE CAN DO SO IN GOOD FAITH, MR. NEVIN IS GRANTED LEAVE TO AMEND TO ALLEGE ULTIMATE FACTS SHOWING THAT EAN HOLDINGS IS LIABLE FOR THE SEPTEMBER 12, 2014 INCIDENT AND THAT THE GRAVES AMENDMENT IS INAPPLICABLE AND/OR UNCONSTITUTIONAL IF APPLICABLE. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER AND TO COMPLY WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR # 5976 =302/HK

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jan-11-2017 Sustained
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
DEFENDANT EAN HOLDINGS, LLC'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT as to the sole cause of action for motor vehicle negligence against it is SUSTAINED with twenty days leave to amend. EAN Holdings' request for judicial notice is granted. The complaint does not allege any ultimate facts showing that EAN Holdings is liable for the September 12, 2014 incident nor does the complaint allege any facts showing that the Graves Amendment is inapplicable to Mr. Nevin's claim against EAN Holdings. If he can do so in good faith, Mr. Nevin is granted leave to amend to allege ultimate facts showing that EAN Holdings is liable for the September 12, 2014 incident and that the Graves Amendment is inapplicable and/or unconstitutional if applicable.
Prevailing party to prepare a form of order and to comply with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR # 5976 =302/hk
